title: ChargebackProductFeatureTypeValues
description:
A chargeback or reversal requested by a merchant, usually due to transaction errors or refunds.

Comment:
Example: A merchant reverses a payment after a product is returned.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Customer-Initiated Chargeback Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A chargeback triggered by the customer due to disputes, unauthorized transactions, or errors.

Comment:
Example: A customer notices a duplicate debit on their account and requests reversal.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Fraud-Related Chargeback Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A chargeback triggered due to suspected fraudulent activity.

Comment:
Example: Transactions made on a stolen credit card are reversed.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Processing Error Chargeback Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A chargeback due to technical or operational errors, such as duplicate charges or incorrect amounts.

Comment:
Example: A POS system charges the same amount twice for one purchase.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Compliance Chargeback Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Regulatory / Compliance Chargeback Feature: A chargeback mandated by regulatory rules or consumer protection laws.

Comment:
Example: A bank reverses a transaction because it violates local banking regulations.
